relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Jacob saw food in Egypt while his sons stared at each other.
He sent them down so the family might live.
Ten brothers went toward Egypt.
Benjamin was held back by fear of another loss.
The sons of Israel joined the hungry world going for grain.
The dream came alive: Joseph’s brothers bowed before him.
Joseph recognized them while hiding himself.
Recognition was one-sided.
Joseph remembered the dreams and tested them as spies.
They pleaded that they had come only for food.
They claimed honesty and brotherhood.
Joseph pressed the accusation.
Their family story emerged: twelve brothers, one gone, one kept with father.
Joseph held them under the test.
Benjamin became the proof demanded.
Truth would be tested through the youngest brother.
They sat in custody three days.
On the third day Joseph offered a path to life.
One would remain while the rest carried grain home.
Benjamin must come for their words to stand.
The old guilt surfaced: they remembered Joseph’s distress and their refusal to listen.
Reuben named the blood-accountability they had avoided.
They did not know Joseph understood every word.
Joseph turned away and wept, then bound Simeon before them.
Grain and hidden silver were placed in their sacks.
They loaded the grain and left.
One found his silver returned.
Fear shook their hearts: what has the Divine done to us?
They brought the report to Jacob.
They described the lord of the land as harsh.
They repeated their claim of honesty.
They named the missing and the youngest.
They explained the test.
Benjamin would unlock Simeon and the land.
The silver appeared again, and fear spread through the house.
Jacob felt every loss converging on him.
Reuben offered responsibility, but clumsily and painfully.
Jacob refused to risk Benjamin, fearing sorrow down to Sheol.
